data.title: Stock market falls ahead of Mag 7 earnings, Fed rate decision
data.description:
Wall Street opened in the red on Wednesday as traders looked forward to earnings from some Magnificent 7 companies and the Federal Reserve’s rate decision.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.2%, the Dow (DJI) -0.2%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.3%. 

Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT), and Amazon (AMZN) will release their results after the bell. 

The Federal Open Market Committee will decide on monetary policy, with rates expected to remain unchanged. The central bank decision is being made as Fed Chair Jerome Powell helms his last meeting, with Fed Chair nominee Kevin Warsh waiting in the wings.

Durable goods rebounded more than expected in March. Housing starts rose in March, while building permits fell.

The U.S. international trade in goods deficit widened in March. The wholesale and retail inventories accelerated M/M in March.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1.2 basis points to 4.37%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) ticked up 0.9 basis points to 3.86%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) added 1.2 basis points to 4.95%.

data.title: Capitol Federal Financial GAAP EPS of $0.16 in-line, revenue of $57.7M beats by $0.73M
data.description:
• Capitol Federal Financial press release (CFFN): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.16 in-line.
  • Revenue of $57.7M beats by $0.73M.
  • an efficiency ratio of 52.45%, an improvement from 53.66% the prior quarter;
  • an operating expense ratio of 1.24%, unchanged from the prior quarter;
  • paid dividends of $15.9 million, or $0.125 per share, including a $0.040 per share special dividend; and
  • repurchased 2,155,481 shares of common stock at an average price of $7.16 per share.

data.title: Graphic Packaging, NextEra Energy to build 250 MW solar plant in Texas
data.description:
Graphic Packaging (GPK) said Wednesday it has signed an agreement with NextEra Energy (NEE) to build a 250 MW solar power plant in west Texas, aiming to ‌source renewable electricity and reduce its greenhouse gas emissions.

Graphic Packaging (GPK) said the solar plant, Selenite Springs Energy Center, would be ​located in the ERCOT power market and is expected to start commercial ‌operations ⁠by year-end 2027.

The company said it will be the sole buyer of the facility's renewable energy attribute certificates, expected to cover 43% of its 2025 electricity usage in the U.S. and Canada.

The deal is Graphic Packaging's (GPK) largest virtual power purchase agreement to date and forms part of its target to cut global Scope 1 and 2 ​emissions by 50.4% ​by 2032, adding that the deal would increase its total ​global purchased renewable electricity to ~49%.
